name: Chantada - Montforte Return to the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve
name_original: Chantada - Montforte Vuelta la Reserva de la Biosfera Ribeira Sacra
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Chantada traverse a diverse landscape in the heart of Galicia, characterized by mountain and riverside scenery. The region features the dramatic Miño Canyon within the Ribeira Sacra, known for its terraced vineyards and the Miño River. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, including climbs towards landmarks like Monte Faro, and routes along the picturesque Asma River. This irregular orography provides a range of cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Chantada The most popular road cycling route is The Cova Miño Return, a 44.4 miles (71.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 13 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Miño River and its steep banks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Chantada - Roderio Return, a difficult 30.1 miles (48.4 km) path. This route navigates through varied terrain with consistent climbs. Local road cyclists also love the Chantada - Montforte Return to the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve, a 59.5 miles (95.8 km) trail leading through the Ribeira Sacra region, often completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Chantada is defined by the Miño Canyon, terraced vineyards, and climbs to viewpoints like Monte Faro. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Chantada are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. More than 130 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Chantada's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Chantada?
A: Chantada offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 18 tours available on komoot. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from river valleys to mountain climbs, providing options for various skill levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Chantada?
A: The region's irregular orography means you can expect varied terrain. Routes often feature consistent climbs, especially towards viewpoints like Monte Faro, and descents through areas like the Miño Canyon. You'll find a mix of quiet asphalt tracks and some natural road surfaces, offering a challenging yet rewarding experience.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Chantada?
A: While many routes in Chantada are rated as moderate to difficult due to the hilly terrain, there are options that cater to different fitness levels. For those seeking a less strenuous ride, look for routes that primarily follow river courses or stick to the flatter sections of the valleys. Currently, komoot lists 6 moderate routes, which might be a good starting point.

Q: What are some challenging road cycling routes for advanced cyclists?
A: Advanced cyclists will find plenty of challenges in Chantada. Routes often feature significant elevation gains, such as the climbs towards Monte Faro. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943439605/chantada-roderio-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chantada - Roderio Return&lt;/a&gt;, which navigates varied terrain with consistent climbs, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939094454/chantada-montforte-return-to-the-ribeira-sacra-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chantada - Montforte Return to the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, a longer trail through the Ribeira Sacra region.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that offer panoramic views?
A: Absolutely. Many routes in Chantada are designed to showcase the region's stunning vistas. The climb to Monte Faro offers panoramic views of the surrounding landscape. Additionally, routes through the Ribeira Sacra and along the Miño Canyon provide breathtaking views of the river and its terraced vineyards. Consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/921520" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte Farelo Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; for expansive scenery.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes in Chantada?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Chantada are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158659" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Cova Riverside Beach – Plaza de Galicia, Chantada loop from Chantada&lt;/a&gt; is a popular circular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20158276" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de Galicia, Chantada – Chantada Town Center loop from Chantada&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I see along road cycling routes?
A: Chantada boasts a rich historical and artistic heritage. Cyclists can encounter Romanesque architecture, including ancient monasteries and churches, along various routes. The village of Belesar, divided by the Miño River, features the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6880090" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belesar Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, originally Roman and part of the Winter Way of the Camino de Santiago. The old town of Chantada itself, with its traditional arcaded streets, is also worth exploring.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks or features to discover while cycling?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through the dramatic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6496544" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribeira Sacra River Valley&lt;/a&gt; and Miño Canyon, known for its terraced vineyards. The picturesque Asma River also offers charming surroundings. For a stunning natural spectacle, some routes may pass near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5016863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Augacaída Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Chantada?
A: Chantada offers good road cycling conditions for much of the year. Spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, ideal for longer rides. Summer can be warm, but routes along the rivers or at higher elevations can offer cooler conditions. Even winter can be an option, though some higher passes might be more exposed.

Q: Is it possible to combine road cycling with exploring local culture, like markets?
A: Absolutely! Chantada is known for its traditional markets held on the 5th and 21st of each month. Planning your ride to coincide with these market days offers a vibrant opportunity to discover local produce and crafts, immersing you in the authentic Galician charm of the region.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the road cycling experience in Chantada?
A: The road cycling experience in Chantada is highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 2 reviews. Over 130 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Chantada's varied terrain, often praising the challenging yet scenic routes through the Ribeira Sacra and the rewarding climbs to viewpoints.

Q: Are there any routes that follow the Camino de Santiago?
A: Yes, the Winter Way of the Camino de Santiago passes through the Chantada region, and portions of its route may coincide with some road cycling paths. This offers a unique opportunity to experience a part of this historic pilgrimage trail while on your bike, adding a cultural and spiritual dimension to your ride.

Q: What are the typical distances and durations for road cycling routes in Chantada?
A: Road cycling routes in Chantada vary significantly in distance and duration to suit different preferences. You can find routes ranging from around 30 miles (48 km) like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e943439605/chantada-roderio-return" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chantada - Roderio Return&lt;/a&gt;, which takes about 2 hours 53 minutes, to longer rides of nearly 60 miles (95 km) such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e939094454/chantada-montforte-return-to-the-ribeira-sacra-biosphere-reserve" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chantada - Montforte Return to the Ribeira Sacra Biosphere Reserve&lt;/a&gt;, typically completed in about 4 hours 47 minutes.
tips: Located between the River Miño Canyon and the Sierra Dorsal Galega, with Mount Faro as its summit, its beautiful old quarter and numerous examples of Romanesque architecture are striking. Considered the "heart of Galicia", it offers a variety of landscapes, mixing mountain and riverside landscapes. Among its attractions, a rich and impressive Historical-Artistic heritage stands out, in which the Romanesque style predominates.
4 km downhill route with a large wide road surface leading to the river.
Steel bridge over the Belesar reservoir over the Miño river in the Ribera Sacra natural park. When the water levels are low you can see the old submerged bridge.

waypoints.name: View of vineyards along Carretera de Chantada
